macro_segments.title: Introduction to Chol HaMoed Work Restrictions and the Criteria for Permissibility

micro_segments.title: Review of Mishna assumptions: Davar ha'avad and Tircha as dual requirements for work on Chol HaMoed
Introduction of Shabbat category debate: Rabba (Choresh) vs. Rav Yosef (Zoreia) on watering and weeding
Extension of Shabbat categories to other areas: Kilayim and Shemita implications

macro_segments.title: The Gemara's Analysis of Watering Fields (Mashkin Beit Hashalchin) on Chol HaMoed and Shemita

micro_segments.title: Distinguishing Chol HaMoed allowances based on Tircha concerns and Pesida (loss)
Philosophical characterization: Chol HaMoed restrictions stem from Tircha, not conceptual Melacha categories
The problem of Shemita: how can any work be allowed when the Torah appears to forbid everything?

macro_segments.title: Abaye's Position: Shemita Nowadays is Rabbinic and Follows Rabbi's View

micro_segments.title: Rabbi's interpretation of Shemita: two Shemitot (land and money) are interdependent
When Shemita of land does not apply, neither does Shemita of money; implying Shemita can be non-biblical in certain times
Post-Churban assumption: Shemita is not biblical due to loss of Kedushat Ha'aretz and absence of Yovel

macro_segments.title: Rava's Position: Only Explicit Avot are Biblical, All Toladot are Rabbinic

micro_segments.title: Rava's core claim: Torah only forbade specific Avot (activities), not their Toladot (derivatives)
Proof from text structure: repetition of verbs (Zeria/Zimira, Katzira/Btzira) indicates limitation to enumerated cases
Challenge from Baraita: blanket prohibition language (Sadcha Lo, Karmecha Lo) and derived activities suggest broader scope
Gemara's resolution: the detailed Baraita is all Rabbinic (midRabbanan), merely using Asmachta from verse

macro_segments.title: Debate on Charisha (Plowing): Whether it is Biblically Forbidden and Whether Violator Receives Lashes

micro_segments.title: Yochanan and Elazar debate: does plowing on Shemita incur lashes (biblical violation) or is it only Rabbinic?
Possible basis: Rabbi Avin's Klal u'Prat u'Klal rule—whether broad Ase (Ushbatas Ha'aretz) overrides specific Lav prohibitions
Gemara's resolution: everyone rejects Rabbi Avin; therefore Klal u'Prat u'Klal applies and charisha could be forbidden biblically
Rava's counter: the Torah's specific enumeration (not using Klal u'Prat u'Klal) proves only those activities are biblically forbidden

macro_segments.title: Rav Dimi's Report from Eretz Yisrael and Two Interpretations of Tosefet (Added Time Period)

micro_segments.title: Rav Dimi's uncertainty about the Tosefet discussion in Beis Medrash: were they debating added activities or added days?
Rebi Elazar's interpretation: Tosefet means additional activities (like Charisha) not explicitly in the verse; biblical or Rabbinic?
Rebi Yochanan's interpretation: Tosefet means additional days before Rosh Hashanah when work is forbidden to prepare fields for Shemita year

macro_segments.title: Mishna and Talmud on Plowing Periods: When Work May Continue Before Shemita Begins (Pesach, Shavuot, Rosh Hashanah)

micro_segments.title: Mishna: Beis Shammai vs. Beis Hillel on orchards (until fruit no longer benefits); Rabbi Shimon standardizes dates (Pesach, Shavuot)
Rabban Gamliel's Beis Din annuls the Pesach and Shavuot restrictions, permitting work even closer to Rosh Hashanah
Problem: How can a later Beis Din override earlier Takkanot of Beis Shammai and Beis Hillel without superior authority?
Solution: The original Takana must have included a Tena'o (stipulation) allowing future override if burden becomes unsustainable

macro_segments.title: Source of Tosefet: Halacha L'Moshe MiSinai (One Month) vs. Rabbinic Addition (Back to Pesach/Shavuot)

micro_segments.title: Core Halacha L'Moshe MiSinai: Eser Netiyot rule allows work up to Rosh Hashanah only in specific tree-density case
Implication from Halacha L'Moshe MiSinai: the special allowance proves all other cases are prohibited, establishing Tosefet as quasi-biblical
Rabbi Yitzchak's resolution: Tosefet of one month is Halacha L'Moshe MiSinai; Rabbinic addition extended it back to Pesach and Shavuot
Rabbinic Tosefet included escape clause; Rabban Gamliel removed the Rabbinic portions, leaving the one-month Halacha L'Moshe MiSinai intact

topical_tags.term: Pesida/Hefsed Revach (loss of profit vs. lack of gain)
Avot vs. Toladot (primary categories vs. derivatives)
Rabbi Avin's rule rejecting Klal u'Prat u'Klal for Ase-Lav combinations
Asmachta (mere support for rabbinic law from verse)
Beis Shammai vs. Beis Hillel (orchards and field work periods)
Rabbi Shimon (standardized dates for work cessation)
Rabban Gamliel and Beis Din (override of earlier restrictions)
Rabbi Yitzchak (reconciliation of HLM and Rabbinic Tosefet)
Rosh Hashanah (Jewish New Year marking Shemita transition)
Mishnah of Bava Metzia (cannot override prior Beis Din without greater authority)
